title: Halimium × cheiranthoides
taxonomy: Halimium × cheiranthoides is classified as a hybrid species within the genus Halimium, which belongs to the family Cistaceae. It is a cross between Halimium molinae and Halimium astrum. The classification of Halimium × cheiranthoides reflects its unique genetic makeup, resulting from the hybridization of these two distinct species. As a hybrid, it exhibits characteristics from both parent species, but its classification as a distinct species within the Halimium genus acknowledges its stable and reproducible traits. The classification of Halimium × cheiranthoides is an example of how botanists recognize and categorize complex relationships between plant species.

intro: Halimium × cheiranthoides, commonly known as Cistus x purpureus or Purple Rockrose, is a hybrid perennial plant species in the family Cistaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and skin protection. The bioactive constituents of Halimium × cheiranthoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
